Brookman: Canada is an amazing country, but we must ensure it stays that way   Some children start life several yards down the track; some children start life at the starting line and too many others start their lives far back from the starting line. Those children, through no fault of their own, spend a lot of their lives trying to catch up, just trying to reach the group who are already on their way the day they arrive. Many do not ever attain that goal and, as a society, it is up to all of us to try to give those children a boost, not to provide a free ride but simply to provide all that we can to overcome the challenges that they have faced.

Opinion: G7 allies can look to Canada as a significant answer to the world's energy problems   As the Group of Seven leaders recently gathered in Germany for their summit meetings, climate change and energy security were on the table for discussion. Given Canada’s unique position as a leading oil and natural gas producer that is taking action to address climate change, Canada’s representatives had every right to be among the loudest voices in the room.

Your letters for June 30, 2022   Golf course Needs Work I played Confederation golf course recently. It’s in terrible shape; the city has allowed dandelions to take over many of the fairways. The ponds are scummy. I can understand not spraying parks, but not spraying fairways is ridiculous. I’m sure there’s a budget for the golf courses. Our mayor and her council are too busy declaring climate emergencies instead of dealing with the day-to-day operations of our city. This used to be an amazing golf course. It could be again with some TLC.

Brooks: Outstanding business leaders honoured at award ceremony   Since 1993, the Distinguished Business Leader Award has been presented to outstanding leaders in the Calgary business community. The prestigious award, co-presented by the Haskayne School of Business and the Calgary Chamber of Commerce, honours individuals whose vision and ethical leadership, sound business sense and commitment to community have marked them as role models for future generations of business leaders. This year’s award gala, held June 23 at the Hyatt Regency, honoured the 29th recipient, Andrea Robertson and the 30th  recipient, Wayne Chiu.
